questions.question: How long do dental fillings last?
questions.answer: With proper care, composite fillings typically last 5-10 years, while amalgam fillings can last 10-15 years or more.

questions.question: Does getting a filling hurt?
questions.answer: No, we use local anesthesia to ensure you're comfortable throughout the procedure. You might feel slight pressure, but no pain.

questions.question: How long does the procedure take?
questions.answer: Most filling procedures take 30-60 minutes per tooth, depending on the size and location of the cavity.

questions.question: Can I eat normally after getting a filling?
questions.answer: You can eat as soon as the anesthetic wears off, typically within a few hours. We recommend waiting 24 hours before eating hard foods.

aftercare.title: Aftercare Instructions
steps: Wait for numbness to wear off before eating
Avoid very hot or cold foods for 24 hours
